meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no version control (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Claude Code Setup to Solve Version Control Issues
content.seo_description: Learn how to use Claude Code and Antigravity to master version control. A beginner-friendly checklist for students building their first AI-powered apps.
content.answer_block: Stop manually tracking code changes. By integrating Claude Code with your development environment, you automate version control, commit history, and branch management. This setup transforms your workflow from chaotic file-copying to a professional, industry-standard engineering process, ensuring your portfolio projects are robust, traceable, and ready for deployment.
content.intro:
You stare at a folder full of 'final_v2_real_final' files, paralyzed by the fear that one wrong keystroke will erase your progress. Every student reaches this breaking point: the realization that manual file management isn't just inefficient—it's a career-limiting bottleneck. You aren't just writing code; you are building the foundation of your future as an engineer.

The relief arrives when you stop fighting the tools and start mastering them. By coupling the rapid logic of Claude Code with the structural precision of Antigravity, you turn version control from a chore into a superpower. This guide is your roadmap to professional-grade code stability.

steps.title: Initialize Git Repository
steps.description: Before you write a single line of code, you must initialize a local Git repository to track your changes systematically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a professional Git repository for this project, create a .gitignore file tailored for Python/Node.js, and configure the initial commit.

steps.title: Configure Claude Code Environment
steps.description: Connect Claude Code to your repository to allow the agent to read your commit history and suggest improvements.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scan my project structure and configure Claude Code to recognize my current working directory as a version-controlled project.

steps.title: Automate Branching Strategy
steps.description: Learn to isolate features using branches, which prevents breaking the main codebase during experimentation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a new branch for the current feature and ensure all subsequent Claude Code edits are tracked within this branch.

steps.title: Context-Aware Commit Messages
steps.description: Use Claude Code to generate meaningful commit messages that explain the 'why' behind your changes, not just the 'what'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the changes I just made and write a concise, conventional-commit style message for the next commit.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "git commit -m 'feat: implement initial logic'"

steps.title: Verify History Integrity
steps.description: Regularly audit your progress to ensure your version history is clean and follows a logical progression.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my commit log and identify any missing documentation or redundant code blocks that should be squashed.

pro_tips: Always use atomic commits: one logical change per commit makes debugging 10x faster.
Ignore your node_modules or venv folders strictly; a clean repo is a professional repo.
Use Claude Code to write your README.md files by pointing it to your codebase structure.
Squash your 'work-in-progress' commits before merging to keep your portfolio history looking clean.
Enable Git hooks to automatically run linter checks before allowing a commit to be finalized.
resources: Git Best Practices for Students: https://git-scm.com/doc
content.next_steps: Once your version control is automated, focus on deploying your first project to Vercel or Railway to see your code live in a production environment.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation to build software that scales. Don't let your code gather dust on a local machine; turn it into a live, version-controlled product. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
